objective: Build a compact staged system for graph-based task execution: first OpenClaw graph-task skill/core, then Obsidian-based visualization/testing, then autonomous completion using existing execution tools.

phases.name: graph-task-core
phases.goal: Define the graph-task schema, expected-result contract, and CLI/OpenClaw skill behavior for human-guided execution.
scope_out: Obsidian plugin implementation beyond minimal compatibility
expected_results: OpenClaw can inspect a graph, identify executable next work, and record outcomes structurally.
A human can review graph state and verify that transitions match the declared contract.

phases.name: obsidian-ops-surface
phases.goal: Use Obsidian as a human-visible graph management and validation surface for project task graphs.
scope_in: visualization approach using existing Obsidian plugins
graph file organization for project notes
test fixtures demonstrating OpenClaw following graph flow
scope_out: advanced bidirectional sync unless justified by tests
expected_results: A project graph can be visualized and inspected in Obsidian.
Jimmy and OpenClaw can test whether execution follows graph semantics correctly.

phases.name: autonomous-completion-loop
phases.goal: Allow OpenClaw to complete a provided graph to termination using existing execution tools like heartbeat and lobster.
expected_results: OpenClaw can drive a graph from ready state to terminal state without manual intervention except on blocking escalation.
The resulting state remains inspectable and resumable.
